top_repeated_values.value: I am a Manager in the Technology, Data & Analytics practice in London with over 14 years of consulting/implementation experience in delivering end to end Oracle transformation programmes mainly in Retail and Utilities domain. I have worked on multiple engagements on various platforms including Oracle ERP cloud,  Oracle Retail suite & Salesforce helping clients through their digital transformation journey, advising them on potential risks and remediation measures while undergoing transformation and controls design to ensure regulatory compliance.

Andy is an MBA with extensive experience in project, programme and portfolio management and business and technology enabled transformation delivery, consulting, and assurance. He has undertaken senior management roles in industry; and advisory and assurance for PwC, PA and Logica-CMG and as an independent consultant.

He has experience across sectors including over 15 years in Financial Services. Andy started his career in IT development and project management in corporate banking services in the City of London; delivered multiple assignments for the UK financial services regulator; and was head of programme management at a UK general insurer.

Andy has extensive working knowledge of change frameworks, including agile solutions development and SAFe and excellent versatility and influencing skills to collaborate with executive boards to design programme and change strategies; mobilise and set up for success.

Marina is a Resilience and Risk Manager within TDR at PwC. She has Business Analyst experience in Regulatory (Operational Resilience & MiFID II), Application Development, Risk, Operations, Business Intelligence and Analytics areas/projects. She has managed global teams introducing, reforming and automating various work processes/systems._x000D_
​_x000D_
She has worked through entire Software Development Lifecycles producing a number of applications, including several QlikView Dashboards for various divisions. She was a Subject Matter Expert (SME) in a global remediation team for End of Life (EOL) systems that pose a significant operational, security and cyber risk to the firm. Her exceptional performance has allowed her to utilise her experience in Project Management and Data Analysis to assist other Agile Project teams outside her own. This led to her promotion at the investment bank to Defects Manager within Transaction Reporting. _x000D_
_x000D_
She now works on FS clients, including investment banks/insurance companies, as well as non FS clients, such as housing associations. She works on the Operational Resilience regulatory program onboarding a Salesforce based platform called Fusion. She is enthused by new challenges and opportunities to work in a variety of industries._x000D_
_x000D_
Marina holds a CBCI, BCS Foundation/Practitioner Qualifications in Business Analysis, Business Analysis Practice, Requirements Engineering and Modelling Business Processes, as well as PSM1, Foundational Prince2, ITIL and M_o_R Qualifications._x000D_

Asif is a Manager in the Technology, Data and Analytics practice. He has worked in various analytical roles, and has experience working with large tier 1 financial institutions including Investment banking and Asset Management clients. 
Previously Asif has worked within financial services programs and has covered a number of banking data governance, data migration and remediation projects gaining expertise in data analysis, business analysis and project management. 
Asif has conducted ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) and migrated data on several occasions for example most recently at Fidelity Asset Managers he migrated fund data into a newly created database forming part of a Strategic Solution (finance software). He also single handedly worked on a project to migrate data from NewEdge to Societe Generale's systems when the two companies merged. He has strong experience working with the Agile methodology, producing user stories, managing scrum boards and in directing development of AWS cloud based solutions.

Andrew is a Manager in PwC’s Technology and Data Analytics practice, specialising in Financial Services. He has over four years of  post-incident / proactive planning and implementation consulting experience, assessing and advising clients in the following areas: Operational and Cyber Resilience; Resource Capability; Service Operations and Capacity Management; Sourcing and External Dependencies; Risk, Incident, and Change Management; and Service Continuity. 

From a resilience perspective, Andrew has assisted PwC clients across all our service offerings, including (among others): programme implementation, client resilience maturity assessments (ranking clients against best practice), response to regulatory inquiry, and internal audit (including deep dive review). Andrew has also been heavily involved in thought leadership in this space, contributing to a number of resilience white papers and the development of tools to expedite client resilience enhancement programmes.

Sam is a collaborative, results-driven Manager in the FS Technology, Data & Analytics practice, specialising in the Insurance sector. Sam has recently completed his Certificate in Insurance with the Chartered Insurance Institute (CII) and is currently studying for his Diploma in Insurance. Sam has had significant experience in digital and data transformation, including:_x000D_
•	high level solution design (data and technology architecture);_x000D_
•	data modelling (data dictionaries, logical and physical data models, data contracts, functional and technical specifications);_x000D_
•	visual intelligence and data reporting;_x000D_
•	data privacy and protection advisory (inc. Europe's GDPR and Thailand's PDPA); and_x000D_
•	data governance and migration reviews._x000D_
_x000D_
Sam has experience building, mentoring and leading talented multi-disciplinary and high performing teams – including large scale programmes overseas, and complex IFRS 17 and actuarial transformations.

Charles is a Technology, Data and Analytics Manager in Financial Services team with lots of experience in Governance, Strategy, Design and Implementation of Technology Controls. This includes aligning to meet regulatory requirements. He also has experience in Data Governance, including data maturity, critical data element strategy, authoritative data sourcing, data accountabilities and RACI frameworks. He has worked on a long-term project addressing gaps in the technology change control environment for a major FS institution following a Section 166 from the FCA. He also has experience in data privacy. His technical skills include SQL, PowerBI, Python and Azure from his client work and his Physics degree. He is keen to continue to build up his FS business experience and technical skills by working on projects in the FS sector. Charles has been commended for his strong work ethic, intuitive ability to step up to required roles and ability to quickly pick up technical skills in his feedback. His strong communication skills, ability to maintain and build business relationships, manage expectations and delegate responsibilities have been recognised by team members on his engagements so far.

Neale is a Manager with a Project Management and Business Analyst background who has demonstrated consistent high performance across a range of projects with x-LoS PwC teams (primarily Deals and Consulting) for Change Solutions. He has excellent team leadership abilities, and has experience with agile environments, change management and digital transformation principles. He has a proven track record of outstanding delivery with PwC teams & building great relationships with clients across a range of industries, from Financial Services to Tech and the Public Sector.

Neale was most recently the PMO lead for the Cutover workstream on a £400m major public-facing technology programme, which he successfully delivered. This required him to coordinate the activities of over 80 companies, report risks and issues and provide status reports to senior programme stakeholders up to and including Deputy Director level and out to senior management teams within organisations, developing strong relationships with them.

Previously, Neale worked with the PwC Deals team on two post-deal clients for a major PE firm. For both clients, an international pharma firm and a global media consultancy, Neale supported project management & performed data analysis for the building of dashboards. For the second, in addition to his other responsibilities, he was also seconded to the client programme manager to assist the set-up of their project portfolio & overall programme governance. For both projects, Neale developed client documentation such as SteerCo decks, performed data analysis and review in excel, ran project management governance such as weekly reports and budgets, & led meetings with key senior stakeholders, including the client CFO. Owing to his strong performance, he was extended by the client in the first role once the main PwC team rolled off the engagement, and was asked for specifically by the client programme manager in the second role to assist programme set-up.

Prior to this, he worked for Consulting teams on several engagements. On one of these, Neale acted as an IT Project Manager (PM) on secondment to a governmental client. He was responsible for overall project delivery on three digital projects, using a range of agile and traditional methodology for each, as well as his BA skills. In the role, he created work packages, oversaw workstreams and managed client teams, suppliers and senior stakeholders, as well as leading and facilitating meetings. He maintained project governance effectively and used his business analyst skills in the development of business cases, requirements documentation and service improvement plans.

Horacio is an experienced IT professional within the Technology, Data & Analytics practice at PwC. He has over 25 years of experience in IT, including in technology and cloud architecture, resilience and security.  

Horacio has led complex technology resilience, security assurance, and regulatory-driven projects, and has provided technology SME support to IT Management and Internal Audit in financial services. He has also conducted technology failure investigations, including for major IT outages and cyber incidents.

Horacio's technical expertise and working experience spans mainframes, on-premise technology and cutting-edge cloud and AI technologies. He has extensive experience on mainframes, which includes z/OS, VM, VSE, RACF, ACF2, Top Secret, CICS, COBOL and DB2. He also explores the latest innovations like z/16 capabilities and modernisation initiatives that leverage these advancements to integrate mainframes with modern architectures, such as containerisation and event-driven architectures.
 
Prior to PwC, Horacio worked at an important bank in IT functions in system operations, application programming, systems analysis, system programming and security administration, with focus on mainframe technology.

I have over 20 years experience in banking and capital markets; with specific focus on data and analytics. Specialising in regulatory compliance, transaction reporting, customer analytics, data analytics, data governance and data-driven transformation programmes.

sample_values:
I have around 10 years experience working in data and specialises in investment banking and asset management. I have experience in data structure, data governance, and data controls, particularly around financial systems.  I have an established track record of delivering high quality, detailed reviews, and working with clients to improve their processes. This includes stakeholder engagement throughout organisations.

I have covered a wide range of transaction reporting regulations, and have led project delivery teams with respect to EMIR, HKMA and MiFID, and have been the SME for two ASIC projects for clients in Australia. Within this role, I have worked with teams across the companies, including operations, technology and compliance. I have also worked on CFTC engagements.

I have in depth expertise of financial products and systems that drive regulatory reporting. As well as technical abilities, I also has experience in project management, documentation, and report writing.

I have a strong background in data processing with experience creating efficient workflows in Alteryx and establishing data architectures. My experience includes developing user-friendly dashboards that provide stakeholders with critical insights. In the realm of operational resilience, I have experience ensuring compliance with regulatory standards such as DORA._x000D_
_x000D_

I am a passionate data analyst dedicated to leveraging my expertise to solve complex technological challenges. With extensive experience in data manipulation, visualization, and machine learning, I thrive on transforming raw data into actionable insights. Recently, I have begun exploring the OpenAI Python library, delving into its capabilities to unlock its full potential in innovative applications.

Yousef is a Manager in our Technology, Data and Analytics practice, with 5+ years of experience effectively managing technology risks for his clients. His leadership, interpersonal skills and stakeholder management allow him to create meaningful client relationships and effectively manage complex programmes and activities across various industries including commercial, financial services and government._x000D_
_x000D_
With a strong background in performing technology control assessments at global retail and multinational investment banks, Yousef has strengthened control environments and significantly enhanced data security measures for numerous organisations. He also provides clients across different industries with recommendations to strengthen data privacy landscapes in M&A deals (pre-sale), and has successfully worked with a multinational investment bank to define a Policy integration strategy for its acquisitions (post-sale).

Yousef is a Manager in our Technology, Data and Analytics practice, with 5+ years of experience effectively managing technology risks for his clients. His leadership, interpersonal skills and stakeholder management allow him to create meaningful client relationships and effectively manage complex programmes and activities across various industries including commercial, financial services and government._x000D_
_x000D_

With a strong background in performing technology control assessments at global retail and multinational investment banks, Yousef has strengthened control environments and significantly enhanced data security measures for numerous organisations. He also provides clients across different industries with recommendations to strengthen data privacy landscapes in M&A deals (pre-sale), and has successfully worked with a multinational investment bank to define a Policy integration strategy for its acquisitions (post-sale).

Previously Asif has worked within financial services programs and has covered a number of banking data governance, data migration and remediation projects gaining expertise in data analysis, business analysis and project management. 
Asif has conducted ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) and migrated data on several occasions for example most recently at Fidelity Asset Managers he migrated fund data into a newly created database forming part of a Strategic Solution (finance software). He also single handedly worked on a project to migrate data from NewEdge to Societe Generale's systems when the two companies merged. He has strong experience working with the Agile methodology, producing user stories, managing scrum boards and in directing development of AWS cloud based solutions.

Andrew works with broad range of clients across a number of industries to help clients manage the risks of using third parties as part of their operations._x000D_
_x000D_
He joined PwC from HSBC and has 18 years’ experience in procurement, supplier risk management and outsourcing, both in consultancy and industry roles. His experience covers operational risk, organisational and third party governance, supply chain resilience and outsourcing regulations._x000D_
_x000D_
Andrew’s deep expertise lies in third party and outsourcing risk and this is combined with practical experience of working in procurement and supplier management roles in industry. Andrew's most recent roles have included_x000D_
* A number of TPRM review, design and implementation programmes, regulatory remediation programmes, transformation and change programmes * Advising clients on industry and regulatory expectations against the EBA / PRA (and other regimes)_x000D_
* Oversight and execution of third party assessment / management programmes, most notably large scale assessment programmes._x000D_
* A number of secondments to clients in key roles._x000D_
*The design of a number of industry standards, including the UK FS "Supplier Assurance Standard", the KY3P standard and Hellios Stage 3 standard._x000D_
_x000D_
At HSBC, prior to PwC, he was responsible for the step change needed to improve the contracts HSBC's global vendor risk management (VRM) programme that helped to make it an industry recognised leading function for a number of years, and continued so today.

top_repeated_values.value:
• Lead programme manager responsible for PMO management and delivery to support client regulatory review.
• Designed project infrastructure and controls, responsible for all facets of managing the project operational governance audit trail.
• Responsible for senior stakeholder engagement and communications.
Performed R&Q, compliance assessment, interview assistance and report writing for the ZIG EQA

Independently rebuilt the Loss Triangles for the project, which profiled claims payments by accident year and payment period.

This assured the accuracy and completeness of the clients triangles so that PwC could perform independent projections.

SME supporting EMIR 3 implementation plan. Included:
> Supporting product working groups
> Regulatory response support
> Cross-regulatory impact assesments

Engagement Manager for MiFID II review. Work contained two phases:

1) Testing completeness and accuracy of regulatory reports. This was done by taking source data and independently deriving reportable transactions and field values, and then reconciling these against end reports. Management of the list of exceptions raised and assisted the client in their investigation and acceptance of issues.

2) Building technical specifications so that the client could build the reporting independently of the third party provider they were currently using. This was to create a challenger report as part of a controls process. We also documented current controls and data ownership of required MiFID II source attributes

SME role for two client engagements in Australia for ASIC transaction reporting regulation. Guidance on general transaction reporting testing processes, along with guidance on business, instrument and regulatory matters

Elected on 3rd May 2018 to represent Churchfields Ward on Redbridge Borough Councillor. Responsibilities include:

1) Policy maker for Council strategy, budget
2) Shadow cabinet member for Children and Young People (education, looked after children, adoption and foster care oversight and policy)
3) Community leader
4) Improving economic, social, environmental problems in the ward 
5) Advocate for local residents who need representation about specific concerns at Council

Calculated net amount of currency that was exposed to FX fluctuations, computing the gains/losses from those exposures over the period of a year.

Worked with the treasury team to build a model to hedge against those exposures to mitigate the risk of any gains/losses due to FX movement.

The model contained assumptions, like customer cancellation rates. Analysis was also segmented by various entities, currency pairs and time periods

Building and testing transaction reports from source to end regulatory report for the below regulations:
EMIR
EMIR2
CFTC
MiFID II

Independently rebuilt an econometric model that calculated the risk free rates to be used by the insurance industry for Solvency 2. 

The model included the calculation of many adjustments, like credit risk and probability of default. The model used the Smith-Wilson method to interpolate and extrapolate yield curves

After rebuilding the model in R using only the Business Requirements Document, we worked with the client to correct any errors in their model.
